news.title: George Kittle, other NFL players push for grass after World Cup resurfacing
news.lede: The World Cup is finished, but NFL players hope grass fields are not. After football venues including MetLife Stadium in New Jersey swapped out synthetic turf for natural grass for the global soccer tournament, big-name players including George Kittle, Breece Hall and Jonathan Taylor urged the NFL to stick with grass. “Grass looks great at MetLife! Can we keep it that way @NFL??

news.title: What's next for World Cup hero Ferran Torres? FC Barcelona faces fight to keep Spain's match-winner
news.lede: One goal can change everything. Just days after scoring the extra-time winner that gave Spain a 1-0 victory over Argentina in the 2026 World Cup Final, Ferran Torres has become one of the hottest names on the European transfer market. The striker's stock has never been higher, and while FC Barcelona is determined to keep him, interest from some of Europe's biggest clubs is threatening to complicate the situation. At the front of that list is Paris Saint-Germain, which is reportedly pushing hard to bring the Spanish international to France ahead of the 2026-27 season.

news.title: CONMEBOL President Claims 2030 World Cup Will Be 64-Team Tournament
news.lede: CONMEBOL president Alejandro Dominguez has claimed on social media that the 2030 World Cup will expand to an unprecedented 64-team tournament. The bold declaration follows confirmation from Gianni Infantino that FIFA plans to evaluate further expansion after the groundbreaking 48-team 2026 edition in North America. CONMEBOL president Dominguez sparked widespread speculation after claiming on social media that the 2030 World Cup will feature 64 participating nations. The centenary edition is scheduled to be co-hosted by Spain, Morocco, and Portugal, alongside three ceremonial opening matches in Argentina, Paraguay, and Uruguay.

news.title: ‘The scrutiny is constant’: referee Anthony Taylor retires from football
news.lede: The 47-year-old took charge of five World Cup matches. Taylor has refereed in the Premier League for 16 years. Anthony Taylor has announced his retirement from elite refereeing after a distinguished career spanning more than two decades and 831 matches. One of England’s most accomplished officials, the 47-year-old officiated 668 matches in the professional game in England.

news.title: World Cup becoming a 64-team tournament would create logistical challenges - so can Gianni Infantino's idea work?
news.lede: This year's World Cup is finally over - and after 48 teams took part, it was a long, long tournament. But just as we bid to say goodbye to the 2026 edition, this competition may be getting even bigger. FIFA president Gianni Infantino says any further expansion of the World Cup for 2030 - where six countries are set to host the tournament - ‌will be discussed now the 2026 World Cup has finished. And one solution FIFA are discussing is a 64-team tournament.

news.title: Eight things we learned from Spain’s 1-0 victory over Argentina in the 2026 World Cup final
news.lede: In yet another battle of attrition for this World Cup, Spain ousted the defending champs, Argentina, 1-0 in a tense, physical, and sometimes sloppy finale. An extra time banger from Ferran Torres was the difference, but it was much deeper than a timely goal. Argentina’s mastery of referees and the clock betrayed them on the biggest stage, while Spain’s consistency was the difference. Here are some things we learned from the World Cup finale.

news.title: Messi's tears and Spain's triumph: was the 2026 World Cup final boring?
news.lede: “La Roja” reclaimed the trophy after 16 years! And Argentina's captain couldn't hold back his emotions. The World Cup in North America has officially ended. Spain became the world champion, beating Argentina in the final (1:0).

news.title: Fact check: Trump wasn't edited out of Spain World Cup photo
news.lede: After Spain won the FIFA World Cup, the Spanish Football Federation posted a photo of the trophy celebrations, raising questions about whether Donald Trump had been edited out. Several posts have been circulating online claiming that US President Donald Trump was edited out of a photo postedby the Spanish Football Federation on its social media accounts. Claim: One post on the social media platform X gained more than 11 million views while making the same claim. "Lol Spain edited Trump out of the photo," the caption read.
